meaning: n.通风孔,出烟孔,出口,(感情等的)发泄v.放出,排出,发泄
examples: She gave vent to her anger and jealousy.
She telephoned her best friend to vent her frustration.
She gave full vent to her feelings in a violent outburst.
The disruption prompted angry BlackBerry users to vent their frustrations on social networking sites like Twitter.
The tattooing became a way to "vent, " he said, in his offseasons a few years ago.
